seoTitle: Calculate a Running Total in Google Sheets
metaDescription: Learn how to calculate a running total in Google Sheets to track cumulative expenses, sales, or any ongoing dataset with simple formulas.
intro: A running total keeps a cumulative sum updated as new data comes in, which is essential for tracking expenses, sales, or any figure that builds over time. This guide covers how to calculate a running total in Google Sheets, including the key formulas and common pitfalls to watch for.

steps.heading: Set up your Google Sheet
steps.body: Organize your data with a clear column for the values you want to accumulate before applying a running total formula.

steps.heading: Understand the formulas for running totals
steps.body: A running total typically uses a formula that adds each new value to the sum of all previous values in the column.

steps.heading: Apply a practical example: sales tracking
steps.body: Use a running total to track cumulative sales over time, giving you an at-a-glance view of performance trends.

steps.heading: Automate your running total
steps.body: Set the formula up so it extends automatically as new rows of data are added, keeping the total current without manual updates.

steps.heading: Avoid common pitfalls
steps.body: Watch for formula reference errors when copying the running total formula down a column, which is a frequent source of mistakes.

faq.question: What is a running total used for?
faq.answer: A running total keeps a cumulative sum of a dataset, which is useful for tracking expenses, sales figures, or any data that requires an ongoing calculation.

faq.question: Is this beginner-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the process is designed for beginners while still being useful for more advanced users automating their data management workflows.

faq.question: Can I automate a running total for real-time updates?
faq.answer: Yes, by setting up the formula correctly, the running total will update automatically as new data is entered.

faq.question: What are common mistakes when calculating a running total?
faq.answer: A common pitfall is incorrect cell referencing when copying the formula down, which can break the cumulative calculation.
